codeReview.summary: The Sync Health Dashboard Backend (F014-BE) is well-implemented and meets the feature requirements. All required APIs are present (getSyncStats, getModuleSyncStatus, getFailedItems, retryFailedItem, retryAllFailed), plus helpful additional utilities (getRetryConfig, getQueueHealth, getHealthDashboardData). IPC handlers are properly wired in main.ts with the syncHealth:* namespace convention, and preload correctly exposes the APIs. electron.d.ts is updated with complete type declarations. Tests cover stats, module status, failed items, retry operations, queue health, and exponential backoff display. The implementation integrates cleanly with existing syncQueueService, retryService, and syncDatabase.

issues.description: In getFailedItems, `getSyncQueueStats()` returns `allFailed.failed` as `totalRetriedCount` but the naming is semantically confusing: `totalRetriedCount` suggests a count of how many times items were retried, while the value returned is actually the total number of failed items. This is a naming/labeling issue that could confuse consumers of the API but does not affect functionality.

getModuleSyncStatus hardcodes failedCount to 0 for every module (`failedCount: 0 // Could be tracked separately if needed`). The feature expectedBehavior says getModuleSyncStatus should return per-module status, but this omits the failed count from the module breakdown. This is acceptable because failed items are available via getFailedItems(module), but consumers of the module breakdown will not see failed counts in the per-module response.

In getFailedItems, `lastAttemptAt` is mapped from `item.timestamp` instead of a dedicated `updated_at` or `last_attempt_at` field. The comment acknowledges this limitation. This means the displayed last attempt time will equal the creation time rather than the actual last retry attempt time.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Backend-worker skill correctly followed: tests were added, IPC handlers use syncHealth:* namespace, preload.ts and electron.d.ts were both updated, and { success, message, data } patterns are used where appropriate. The worker added helpful supplementary APIs (getRetryConfig, getQueueHealth, getHealthDashboardData) that go beyond the strict feature list but improve the UI implementation experience for F015-UI.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/main/syncHealthDashboard.ts exports 8 functions; main.ts lines 1861-1913 register all 8 IPC handlers; preload.ts lines 943-1037 expose renderer APIs; electron.d.ts lines 943-1053 declare TypeScript types.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature implementation correctly followed the documented IPC channel naming convention: `syncHealth:getStats`, `syncHealth:getModuleSyncStatus`, etc. The module sync status correctly uses the sync state values defined in the architecture (`synced`, `pending`, `error`, `not_configured`). Error messages are user-facing in Indonesian as required by AGENTS.md.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/main/main.ts line 1864+ uses `ipcMain.handle('syncHealth:getStats', ...`; src/main/syncHealthDashboard.ts line 278 returns message 'Item tidak ditemukan'.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The tests avoid real sql.js database instantiation by mocking syncDatabase, syncQueueService, and retryService. This is a practical pattern when the underlying database module is difficult to mock globally. It ensures fast, isolated unit tests but means the tests don't exercise real SQL queries—relying instead on contract mocks. This is a reasonable trade-off given the test infrastructure in this codebase.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/main/syncHealthDashboard.test.ts mocks all three dependencies with jest.mock at the top of the file.

summary: F014-BE passes review. The Sync Health Dashboard Backend is complete, correctly wired through IPC/preload/types, and backed by a focused unit-test suite. There are no blocking issues; only minor non-blocking concerns around `failedCount` hardcoding to 0 in module status and slightly confusing `totalRetriedCount` naming in getFailedItems.
